subject: [PATCH 1/2] btrfs: inline enable_quota helper in test 301

reviews.author: Zorro Lang
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Zorro Lang expressed amusement at the patch title, which jokingly suggested that fstests might delete itself during execution.

Reviewer noted that this patch is unrelated to the second part of the submission and suggested reviewing them separately.
reviews.raw_body:
Hahah, to be honest, the title '[PATCH 0/2] squota delete fstests' gave me
a minor heart attack. I thought it was a critical bug report about fstests
accidentally deleting itself while running :-D

---

This change doesn't seem related to patch 2/2. I think we can review
these 2 patches separately.

For this patch, I'm fine with or without it. If the btrfs list prefers
to have it, or move it to be a common helper, I'm good with that.

Thanks,
Zorro

2026-05-14.patch_summary: This patch simplifies the btrfs test suite by inlining two calls to enable quota functionality, eliminating a helper function and reducing unnecessary complexity.

2026-05-13.patch_summary: This patch inlines the enable_quota helper function in test 301, removing a dead-weight dispatch and indirection to directly call 'btrfs quota enable --simple'. The change is limited to this specific test and does not affect other parts of the btrfs codebase.

reviews.author: Boris Burkov (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that his original patch submission was unnecessary as a series and agreed it could be sent separately.
reviews.raw_body:
I pulled this into a separate patch based on Filipe's feedback on the
old version I failed to get in. Agreed it doesn't need to be a series,
just felt related enough to send in a batch, due to the context.

Thanks for taking a look,
Boris

2026-05-15.patch_summary: This patch simplifies the btrfs test suite by inlining two calls to enable quota functionality, eliminating a helper function and reducing unnecessary complexity.

reviews.author: Zorro Lang
reviews.summary: Reviewer Zorro Lang noted that since the two patches are independent, one can be merged first as long as it receives an Ack, even if the other patch is still being questioned.
reviews.raw_body:
Don't worry. What I mean is that as long as one of these two patches gets an
Ack first, even if the other is still being questioned, we can merge it first.
After all, they are independent of each other.

2026-05-16.patch_summary: This patch simplifies the btrfs test suite by inlining two calls to enable quota functionality, eliminating a helper function and reducing unnecessary complexity.

2026-05-18.patch_summary: This patch simplifies the btrfs test suite by inlining two calls to enable quota functionality, eliminating a helper function and reducing unnecessary complexity.

reviews.author: Anand Jain
reviews.summary: Reviewer Anand Jain found the patch acceptable and approved it, giving a Reviewed-by tag.
reviews.raw_body:
looks good.

Reviewed-by: Anand Jain <asj@kernel.org>

Thanks.

2026-05-19.patch_summary: This patch simplifies the btrfs test suite by inlining two calls to enable quota functionality, eliminating a helper function and reducing unnecessary complexity.
